This is my first storm! New to Florida, in the Davenport Kissimmee area. When people keep saying we’re going to get it too, should I be worried and heading out? You are all terrifying me

find out your evacuation zone, and plan accordingly with that info, if you are in zone A , do not wait to find a place to stay, roads get backed up quickly, and gas gets scarce along main roadways. If you aren’t in a major flood zone, fill up your gas tank, have some water, keep your phone and other electronics on the chargers until you loose power. Power banks are a life saver. Pre pack your vehicle with essential and creature comforts that you would want beforehand in case you need to leave in a hurry. If you have pets make sure you have extra food bowls and water for them as well. This one with come and go fast. It’s definitely scary, but if you stay prepared and ready, you will be ok!

if you don’t already, follow mikes weather page, he is good at giving you clear info, that has been spot on for a lot of the storms I’ve been through.
Pro tip, if you think your residents or area are unsafe, don’t stay put if you can get a hotel or something, rescues happen after the storm dies down, not during. It’s not at all fun to be in a house crumbling around you and no one coming to help for possibly hours or even days after the fact because roads are blocked or flooded.
Another tip, don’t read these comments. Some of them know well what they are talking about, but most do not, follow what Dennis is saying. Reading the comments can make your anxiety skyrocket for what could be no reason to panic.
I don’t think your in a flood zone. Just a lot of wind. Get water and food. Might lose power. Buckets of water to flush toilets. Fill your bath tubs with water
